contract.requirement_text: Normative requirement statement derived from the canonical feature title.
contract.owner: Feature domain owner from the feature-map ledger.
contract.acceptance_criteria: Feature-map acceptance test or the default served-state evidence gate.
contract.status: Current served_state: served, conditional, partial, library, or roadmap.
contract.ga_served_scope: Explicit GA served-denominator scope: in_ga for fully served rows, out_of_ga for residual conditional/partial/library/roadmap rows.
contract.ga_scope_reason: Human-readable residual reason copied from the feature-map ledger when ga_served_scope is out_of_ga.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Certificate inventory.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers list/filter/empty/error/rate-limit states and health dashboard rendering; the served CAP-DISC-07 test proves estate-wide expiry/source health includes imported and discovered certificates.
items.backend_status: served

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Network discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI and API tests must prove a tenant can create a network source, schedule it, queue a run, execute the served outbox worker, record a finding, surface the discovered certificate, and render CAP-DISC-06 continuous monitoring posture without claiming unsupported source kinds as fully executed; CAP-NHI-03 served tests prove AD/cloud service-account discovery records metadata-only findings.
items.backend_status: served network source/run/finding control plane with outbox-executed TLS scan, certificate recording, continuous monitoring rollup, and CAP-NHI-03 service-account findings

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Agent-based discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests cover CAP-DISC-02 agent ReportInventory over mTLS producing discovery findings, graph nodes, discovery events, API capability metadata, trust-store collector findings, and private-key-material findings without key bytes; Vitest covers agent list, enrollment-token creation, endpoint-discovery capability rendering, empty fleet, and stale-agent warning states.
items.backend_status: served agent enrollment, mTLS heartbeat/renewal, and metadata-only filesystem, PKCS#11, Windows-store, Kubernetes Secret, trust-store, and private-key-material inventory reports into discovery findings plus credential graph

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SSH credential discovery & inventory.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests prove ssh discovery sources queue an outbox-executed non-invasive host-key scan, record metadata-only ssh_key findings, normalize into NHI inventory, and never authenticate or expose key bytes; private-key-material discovery reports metadata-only private_key findings over the agent channel.
items.backend_status: served discovery control-plane source/run/finding records, outbox-executed SSH host-key scans, NHI inventory normalization, and served agent private-key-material reports; privileged SSH trust mutation remains a separate explicit-confirmation workflow

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Agentless cloud certificate discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests prove a cloud_certificate run inventories ACM-compatible certificates through credential references, records discovery findings and certificate inventory, and never stores inline cloud credentials; UI/API tests prove source/run/finding rendering stays metadata-only.
items.backend_status: served discovery control-plane source/run/finding records plus outbox-executed AWS ACM, Azure Key Vault, and GCP Certificate Manager enumeration with credential references

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Secret store discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests prove cloud_secret discovery executes AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, and HashiCorp Vault KV through read-only provider APIs, records metadata-only certificate findings, and never returns secret values; UI/API tests prove secret_store discovery sources, runs, and findings never render, store, or submit secret values.
items.backend_status: served cloud_secret source/run/finding execution for AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, and HashiCorp Vault KV; generic secret_store records remain metadata-only for connector-supplied stores

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide API key/token inventory.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go test proves api_key source/run/finding execution normalizes API keys, cloud access keys, CI/CD tokens, OAuth refresh tokens, and PATs without storing token values; Vitest proves the Discovery source form submits metadata-only token observations.
items.backend_status: served metadata-only api_key source/run/finding execution for API keys, tokens, and PATs plus access-admin API token metadata/list/create/revoke

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Certificate Transparency monitoring.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test proves a CT-log fixture certificate for a watched domain creates a ct_unexpected_issuance finding and dispatches an outbox-backed alert; API, CLI, and UI expose CT watchlist/log configuration, checkpoints, findings, and run queueing.
items.backend_status: served dedicated CT monitoring API/CLI/UI watchlist management plus ct_log discovery source/run/finding execution with outbox-backed unexpected-issuance alerts

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Drift detection.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test proves a watched credential whose fingerprint drifts creates a credential_drift finding, the dedicated drift remediation dashboard lists the finding with action guidance, and an operator decision records discovery.finding.triage_changed audit evidence through the served API and UI.
items.backend_status: served drift discovery source/run/finding execution with outbox-backed drift alerts plus dedicated drift remediation API/CLI/UI dashboard and operator decision workflow

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Credential risk scoring.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers risk ranking, factor explanation, empty state, filtering, navigation from risk row to graph/audit/certificate evidence, the CAP-POST-05 contextual priorities panel, the CAP-POST-01 over-privilege panel, the CAP-POST-02 stale/dormant/orphaned panel, the CAP-POST-03 static-credential panel, and the CAP-POST-04 exposure panel. Served Go tests prove /api/v1/risk/contextual-priorities ranks credentials with graph blast-radius and CBOM crypto context, /api/v1/nhi/posture/overprivilege analyzes managed and discovered NHIs and returns usage-driven least-privilege recommendations, /api/v1/nhi/posture/stale detects stale, dormant, unused, and orphaned NHIs, /api/v1/nhi/posture/static-credentials detects long-lived/static credentials, and /api/v1/nhi/posture/exposure detects internet-exposed/insecure-deployment NHIs without leaking credential material.
items.backend_status: served credential risk scoring plus CAP-POST-01 NHI over-privilege posture, CAP-POST-02 stale/dormant/orphaned NHI posture, CAP-POST-03 static-credential posture, CAP-POST-04 exposure/insecure-deployment posture, and CAP-POST-05 contextual blast-radius prioritization

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Cryptographic discovery & observability (CBOM).
items.acceptance_criteria: internal/server/cbom_served_test.go drives POST /api/v1/cbom/scans over a real TLS endpoint and host config, then reads /api/v1/cbom/assets with FIPS 203/204/205 migration progress.
items.backend_status: served-cbom-scan-and-inventory

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide CA-agnostic outbound issuance.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served CAP-ISS-11 test covers request-only principal denied from self-issue, distinct approver approval, successful signer-backed issue, certificate inventory projection, and event evidence; external CA served tests cover configured backends and outbox idempotency; CA Hierarchy Vitest covers direct external-CA issuance through the typed client with outbox-pending and external-ca-issued browser states; CAP-K8S-07 execution tests prove public CA bundles are distributed to namespace ConfigMaps and private-key PEM fails closed.
items.backend_status: served end-to-end for built-in self-service issuance, configured external CA registry issuance, external CA browser outbox-pending/issued states, CAP-K8S-04 native Kubernetes CertificateSigningRequest support, and CAP-K8S-07 TrustBundle CA-bundle distribution

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Private/enterprise CA hierarchy management.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served tests create online root/intermediate authorities, import an existing signer-backed CA chain, drive the offline-root import plus offline-signed intermediate workflow through /api/v1/ca/*, prove /api/v1/ca/discovery enumerates public upstream CAs, private upstream CAs, and imported CA hierarchy authorities, prove CAP-CA-03 zero-downtime rotation keeps the predecessor issue URL live while signing through the successor, and prove CAP-CA-06 CA renewal/re-key mints a fresh signer-backed CA while keeping both old and new issue URLs live.
items.backend_status: served

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Certificate profiles & registration-authority model.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers valid profile create, invalid JSON, server validation error, version display, and identity issuance using selected profile.
items.backend_status: served

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide ACME Renewal Information (ARI).
items.acceptance_criteria: Served lifecycle test proves a deployed short-lived certificate renews on its ARI window even when outside the fixed renew_before threshold.
items.backend_status: served via ACME renewal-info plus lifecycle scheduler consumption for trstctl-issued deployed X.509 identities

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide X.509 revocation infrastructure.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers revoke confirmation, reason selection, success, failure, OCSP/CRL unavailable, audit link, the Certificates page CRL distribution status, rogue/non-compliant certificate posture, and CT submission queueing; served tests prove CAP-REV-05 detection and CAP-REV-06 CT add-pre-chain/add-chain through the outbox worker.
items.backend_status: served for trstctl hierarchy with OCSP, full/sharded/delta CRL distribution artifacts, outbox-backed CT precert+cert log submission, and CAP-REV-05 rogue/non-compliant certificate posture

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide HSM integration.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served tests generate, rotate, revoke, and zeroize managed keys through the real API against LocalStack AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault HSM, GCP Cloud KMS, and a PKCS#11 HSM lifecycle backend; real AWS KMS runs opportunistically when AWS_* credentials are present. TestServedInHSMNonExtractableGenerationCAPKEY04 proves the generate route returns only public metadata plus extractable=false for an opaque HSM handle. TestEditionsEndpointServesCAPKEY03ValidatedModulePath proves the canonical editions route serves the FIPS validated-module path and external product CMVP residual. UI never exposes key material and displays only public metadata/health/posture for configured backends.
items.backend_status: served AWS SDK v2-backed AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault HSM, GCP Cloud KMS, and PKCS#11 HSM managed-key custody via /api/v1/managed-keys; LocalStack acceptance covers AWS generate/rotate/revoke/zeroize; served CAP-KEY-02 acceptance covers Azure/GCP generate/rotate/revoke/zeroize; served CAP-KEY-01 acceptance covers PKCS#11 generate/rotate/revoke/zeroize; served CAP-KEY-04 acceptance covers in-HSM non-extractable generation with extractable=false and no private material returned; served CAP-KEY-03 posture is exposed on /api/v1/editions and Platform with the FIPS 140-3 Go Cryptographic Module build path and external product CMVP residual; SoftHSM native binding remains cgo-proven
